headline: Wealth Tax Proposal on Top 0.1% Stirs Political Firestorm
context: Deputy Finance Minister Ahmad Maslan proposed a 2% annual wealth tax on individuals with net assets exceeding RM50 million in February 2026, potentially raising RM8 billion from approximately 3,200 ultra-wealthy Malaysians.

cards.big: 2% wealth tax on 3,200 ultra-rich Malaysians could raise RM8 billion — equal to entire SARA cash transfer budget
cards.sub: Top 0.1% hold RM412 billion in declared assets — actual wealth likely 2-3x higher including offshore holdings

cards.big: Malaysia's top 1% owns 33% of national wealth while bottom 50% holds only 8.4% — gap widened 12% since 2015
cards.sub: No inheritance tax, no capital gains tax on shares, no wealth tax — Malaysia effectively taxes labour, not capital

cards.big: 3,200 affected individuals include 28 billionaires and 890 families controlling GLCs, plantations, and property empires
cards.sub: Political donations from ultra-wealthy to all major parties create structural resistance to wealth taxation

cards.big: Implementation requires national wealth registry that Malaysia doesn't have — LHDN tracks income, not assets
cards.sub: Norway's wealth tax works because of 100% asset transparency — Malaysia's property and share registries are fragmented

cards.big: A country that taxes workers' salaries at 30% but taxes billionaires' wealth at 0% has its priorities inverted
Should Malaysia build a national asset registry as the first step toward any wealth or inheritance tax implementation?
